assertions.description: Notification API is exposed — the surface the on-device classifier observes.
Notification.permission is a string ('default' | 'granted' | 'denied') unaffected by the on-device classifier.
navigator.serviceWorker is exposed — push notifications targeted by the classifier flow through SW registrations.
ServiceWorkerRegistration.prototype.pushManager exists (entry point for the push channel evaluated for abuse).
The abuse classifier intentionally has no JS surface — there is no navigator.abusiveNotificationStatus or similar to query.
Notification.requestPermission remains a function (the classifier may revoke under the hood, but the prompt API is unchanged).
